channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: U.S. crude oil falls below $100 per barrel after Trump says Iran talks in final stages
item.description: Trump told reporters that the administration was in the "final stages" of negotiations with Iran, according to a pool report.

item.title: Nvidia earnings live updates: The key numbers to watch for in the report
item.description: Nvidia is reporting its first-quarter results after the market closes. The conference call with analysts is scheduled for 5 p.m. ET.

item.title: Fed officials see rate hike ahead if inflation stays elevated, minutes show
item.description: A majority of officials anticipated that interest rate increases would be necessary if the Iran war continued to aggravate inflation.

item.title: OpenAI to confidentially file for IPO as soon as Friday: Source
item.description: OpenAI is working with banks including Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley.

item.title: House approves breakthrough housing bill in a win for investors
item.description: The U.S. House approved an updated housing affordability bill after removing industry-opposed requirements on selling build-to-rent homes.

item.title: Spiking yields are spooking the stock market. These equities win when rates rise
item.description: Some defensive names that are more closely correlated to the 10-year Treasury yield can fare well in a higher rate environment.

item.title: Zuckerberg's Meta layoffs memo: 'Success isn't a given' in the AI era
item.description: Meta on Wednesday commenced its latest round of layoffs affecting about 10% of the company's workforce, equating to roughly 8,000 jobs.

item.title: Cheap AI could derail OpenAI and Anthropic's IPOs
item.description: Chinese AI labs are matching American frontier capability at a fraction of the cost.

item.title: Short sellers see opportunity as chip stocks rally breaks, bears hold firm into Nvidia earnings
item.description: Despite a pullback across semi shares in recent sessions, traders betting against some of the industry's biggest names have shown little sign of retreating.

item.title: Airbnb adds hotels, car rentals. Chesky says app can become an 'Amazon for services'
item.description: Airbnb on Wednesday added new services and independent hotels to its platform in its latest expansion move in the travel industry.

item.title: Jeff Bezos says there is ‘no truth’ to the ‘buy borrow die’ tax strategy
item.description: The "buy, borrow, die" strategy refers to the practice of wealthy founders or investors borrowing against their assets and using the loan proceeds as income.

item.title: Bezos brushes off concerns of an AI bubble: 'You shouldn't worry about it'
item.description: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos shrugged off concerns of a looming AI bubble, saying the massive investments will ultimately drive the technology forward over time.

item.title: Crypto companies are trying to leave the hype cycle for a more disciplined phase, earnings show
item.description: Crypto companies spent years monetizing volatility. Now they're trying to survive without it.

item.title: Former Cuban President Raul Castro charged with murder in U.S.
item.description: The indictment comes as Trump pushes for a regime change in Cuba, where Castro's communists have been in charge since Fidel Castro led a revolution in 1959.

item.title: Jeff Bezos says bottom half of earners should pay zero in income taxes
item.description: Amazon Executive Chairman Jeff Bezos on Wednesday called for zero federal income taxes on the bottom half of earners.

item.title: Jan. 6 police officers sue Trump to block $1.8B 'lawfare' fund
item.description: The Department of Justice agreed to create the fund to settle a $10 billion lawsuit by President Trump over the leak of his tax records by an IRS employee.

item.title: Wendy's taps former Potbelly CEO to lead struggling burger chain
item.description: Nelson Peltz's Trian Fund Management is seeking funding to take Wendy's private, according to a Financial Times report.

item.title: Trump's face doesn't belong on U.S. passport, senators tell Rubio
item.description: The State Department announced a special edition U.S. passport that will feature President Donald Trump's likeness.

item.title: Something very unusual is happening with Nvidia options
item.description: Ahead of earnings, NVDA's short-dated calls are trading at a premium to puts.

item.title: This real estate stock is setting up for a move higher despite the rise in interest rates, charts show
item.description: Frank Cappelleri takes a look at the real estate sector.

item.title: Stocks rally and oil falls. Plus, the implications of Samsung averting a strike
item.description: Every weekday, the Investing Club releases the Homestretch; an actionable afternoon update just in time for the last hour of trading.

item.title: Considering the Chase Sapphire Reserve's 150,000-point bonus? Here are 4 easy-to-miss reasons it's a great deal
item.description: The Sapphire Reserve is more than just a big bonus. Carefully considering these factors can help you easily determine if it's a good fit for you.

item.title: UK announces 'historic' trade deal with Gulf states in G7 first
item.description: The U.K. is the first G7 country to secure a trade agreement with the GCC.

item.title: Authentic Brands Group expects IPO in next 12 months as new CEO steps in, founder tells CNBC
item.description: Authentic Brands Group, the firm behind Reebok and Champion, is bringing in a public company veteran as its next CEO, signaling it's readying for an IPO.

item.title: Bezos says 2-3 year timeline for space data centers is a 'little ambitious'
item.description: Space companies are racing to make data centers in space a reality, as artificial intelligence skyrockets demand for energy and land resources.

item.title: Target beats Wall Street estimates, hikes sales outlook as shoppers start to return
item.description: Target has been struggling with a sales slump and decreased customer traffic, though CEO Michael Fiddelke has said the retailer is poised for a turnaround.

item.title: Lowe's beats Wall Street expectations against 'challenging' housing backdrop
item.description: Lowe's beat Wall Street expectations for its fiscal first-quarter earnings and reaffirmed its full-year guidance.

item.title: Fintech firm Mercury hits $5.2 billion valuation after funding round, up 49% in 14 months
item.description: Mercury has emerged as one of a select group of fintech firms, like Ramp and Stripe, that continued to thrive after the collapse of pandemic-era valuations.

item.title: More car buyers are shifting to EVs — but the reasons why are nuanced
item.description: Trade in data from Edmunds shows more car buyers are choosing EVs, as fuel prices spike. But other factors might be holding back demand.

item.title: Could Trump Accounts be a model for Social Security? Here's what experts say
item.description: New Trump Accounts for children could "become Social Security personal accounts," Sen. Ted Cruz, R-Texas, recently said. Here's what experts say.
